revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24432061637|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:御霊神社 (藤沢市宮前)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Goryō shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa, Kanagawa).jpg|prefecture={{ill|Fujisawa|qid=Q386661|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.33736111|N|139.50841667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}}} {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa)|ja|御霊神社 (藤沢市宮前)||12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa)|qq=}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Goryō Shrine''' (御霊神社) |image = {{ill|Goryo shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa, Kanagawa).jpg|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa, Kanagawa).jpg|1=Goryo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489356}}
Shrine building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|14.50|N|139|30|30.30|E|qq=}}|qq=}} |Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} Miyamae 560 |ISO = JP-14 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 20 |lat_sec = 14.50 |lng_deg = 139 |lng_min = 30 |lng_sec = 30.30 |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (さわらしんのう)
{{ill|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|en|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|qid=Q1154948|lt=Kamakura Gengorō Kagemasa|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa}} (かまくらごんごろうかげまさ)
{{ill|Prince Kuzuhara|ja|葛原親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kuzuhara|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620877}} (くずはらしんのう)
{{ill|King Takami|ja|高見王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Takami|1=Takami-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672827}} (たかみおう)
{{ill|Taira no Takamochi|en|Taira no Takamochi|qid=Q11483791|lt=King Takamochi|1=Taira no Takamochi}} (たかもちおう) |Sacred Object = |Shrine Rank = |Established = {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tenkyo|1=Tengyō}} 3rd year (940) |Main Hall = |Alternate Name = |Saddles, etc. = |Annual Festival = Second Sunday of September |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | image = Goryō shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa, Kanagawa).jpg }} '''Goryō Shrine''' (御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Miyamae, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}.[[ja:御霊神社 (藤沢市宮前)]] == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (さわらしんのう) * {{ill|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|en|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|qid=Q1154948|lt=Kamakura Gengorō Kagemasa|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa}} (かまくらごんごろうかげまさ) * {{ill|Prince Kuzuhara|ja|葛原親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kuzuhara|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620877}} (くずはらしんのう) * {{ill|King Takami|ja|高見王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Takami|1=Takami-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672827}} (たかみおう) * {{ill|Taira no Takamochi|en|Taira no Takamochi|qid=Q11483791|lt=King Takamochi|1=Taira no Takamochi}} (たかもちおう) == History == In the 3rd year of {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tenkyo|1=Tengyō}} (940), {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|en|Taira no Yoshifumi|qid=Q11483528|lt=Muraoka Gorō Yoshifumi|1=Taira no Yoshifumi}} enshrined for the purpose of praying for the defeat of {{ill|Taira Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by invoking the enshrinement from the {{ill|Kamigorei Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|lt=Goryō Shrine|lt_ja=御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigorei Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359018}} of Rakuchu ({{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}} {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}} {{ill|Kamigorei-mae Street|ja|上御霊前通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigorei-mae Street|1=Kamigoryōmae Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359017}}). Later, {{ill|Kamakura Kagemasa|ja|鎌倉権五郎景政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉景正|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1154948}}, {{ill|Prince Kuzuhara|ja|葛原親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kuzuhara|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620877}}, {{ill|King Takami|ja|高見王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Takami|1=Takami-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672827}}, and {{ill|King Takamochi|ja|高望王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Takamochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平高望|1=Taira no Takamochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483791}} were added to the enshrined deities. == Subshrines == * {{ill|The Twelve Heavenly Kings|ja|十二天王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Twelve Heavenly Kings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135504457}} * {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|qid=Q7543689|lt=Smallpox Deity|1=Smallpox demon}} * Yatake {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|qid=Q719665|lt=Great Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} – also written as Ori-sasa Yatake Inari or Sasa-ori Yatake Inari. It is said that {{ill|Kamakura Kagemasa|ja|鎌倉権五郎景政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉景正|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1154948}}, during the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|qid=Q1187411|lt=Later Three Years' War|1=Gosannen War}}, had an arrow rebound after one of his eyes was struck, defeating his enemy. When that arrow was driven into the ground, bamboo sprang up, and thus Yatake Inari was enshrined.{{ill|Fujisawa Place Names 3rd Edition|ja|藤沢の地名 第3版|lt=Fujisawa Place Names, 3rd Edition, 1997|lt_ja=『藤沢の地名』1997th year 、第3版。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Place Names 3rd Edition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 99, 100 pages. * {{ill|Seven-Faced Shrine|ja|七面宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Faced Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Branch Shrines == Owing to its proximity to the ruins of Muraoka Castle—the former residence of {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|en|Taira no Yoshifumi|qid=Q11483528|lt=Muraoka Gorō Yoshifumi|1=Taira no Yoshifumi}}—located in Muraoka Castle Ruins Park (28-2 Muraoka Higashi 3-chōme, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}) ({{Coord|35|20|40.4|N|139|30|27.8|E|region:JP|name=村岡城址|qq=}}), there are 16 shrines in the prefecture said to have branched off from this shrine via {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|qid=Q195793|lt=Branching of Spirit|1=Bunrei}}. These include, for example, the shrine in Kamakura City – {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|御霊神社 (鎌倉市)|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt_ja=御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Kamakura)|1=Goryō Shrine (Kamakura City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489364}}, the one in Hayama Town – {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Hayama)|ja|御霊神社 (葉山町)|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt_ja=御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Hayama)|1=Goryō Shrine (Hayama Town)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489362}}, and Kumano Shrine in Ōoba, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}, also identified with the former {{ill|Ooba Shrine|ja|大庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooba Shrine|1=Ooba Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11435394}}, as well as other shrines of the same lineage such as {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|大庭神社#熊野神社（藤沢市大庭）|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}. File:Historic site of Muraoka Castle, Fujisawa, Kanagawa.jpg|Muraoka Castle Ruins File:Goryou jinnja 002.jpg|Goryō Shrine (Kamakura City) File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma03.JPG|Kumano Shrine (Ōoba, Fujisawa City) == Festivals == * Annual Festival: Second Sunday of September * Yuhana Kagura == Location Information == ; Location : {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} Miyamae 560 ; Transportation :* Bus :** Muraoka Miyamae ({{ill|Enoden Bus|ja|江ノ電バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoden Bus|1=Enoden Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11550595}}) == Other == * Hatadateyama – The hill extending east of Goryō Shrine, also known as Heitan, is so named because {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qid=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} raised a white flag here to rally his troops.{{ill|Fujisawa Place Names 3rd Edition|ja|藤沢の地名 第3版|lt=Fujisawa Place Names, 3rd Edition, 1997|lt_ja=『藤沢の地名』1997th year 、第3版。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Place Names 3rd Edition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 100 pages. == References == {{Footnote Help|qq=}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Note"|qq=}} '''Sources''' {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book |language=Japanese |author= |editor=Japan Place Names Institute |title=Fujisawa Place Names |edition=3rd edition |date=1997-03-31 |publisher=Fujisawa City Department of Civic Activities, Local Autonomy and Cultural Affairs |isbn= |ref=Fujisawa Place Names 3rd Edition|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県の神社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588941}} == External Links == {{Osm box|w|258439722|qq=}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=514&cd=1206001&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Goryo Shrine|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo Shrine|qq=}}] - Kanagawa Prefecture Shinto Shrine Office {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{citation needed|ja|御霊神社|version=104115450|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御霊神社]] [[ja:%E5%BE%A1%E9%9C%8A%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E8%97%A4%E6%B2%A2%E5%B8%82%E5%AE%AE%E5%89%8D)]] {{translated page|ja|御霊神社 (藤沢市宮前)|version=101568321|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Goryō Shrine''' (御霊神社) |image = {{ill|Goryo shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa, Kanagawa).jpg|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa, Kanagawa).jpg|1=Goryo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489356}}
Shrine building {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|14.50|N|139|30|30.30|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} Miyamae 560 |ISO = JP-14 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 20 |lat_sec = 14.50 |lng_deg = 139 |lng_min = 30 |lng_sec = 30.30 |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (さわらしんのう)
{{ill|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|en|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|qid=Q1154948|lt=Kamakura Gengorō Kagemasa|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa}} (かまくらごんごろうかげまさ)
{{ill|Prince Kuzuhara|ja|葛原親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kuzuhara|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620877}} (くずはらしんのう)
{{ill|King Takami|ja|高見王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Takami|1=Takami-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672827}} (たかみおう)
{{ill|Taira no Takamochi|en|Taira no Takamochi|qid=Q11483791|lt=King Takamochi|1=Taira no Takamochi}} (たかもちおう) |Sacred Object = |Shrine Rank = |Established = {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tenkyo|1=Tengyō}} 3rd year (940) |Main Hall = |Alternate Name = |Saddles, etc. = |Annual Festival = Second Sunday of September |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan |image= Goryo shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa, Kanagawa).jpg |qq= https://ja.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E3%83%95%E3%82%A1%E3%82%A4%E3%83%AB |image= File:Gory%C5%8D_shrine_(Miyamae,_Fujisawa,_Kanagawa).jpg }} '''Goryō Shrine''' (御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Miyamae, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (早良親王) * {{ill|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|en|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|qid=Q1154948|lt=Kamakura Gengorō Kagemasa|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa}} (鎌倉権五郎景政) * {{ill|Prince Kuzuhara|ja|葛原親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kuzuhara|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620877}} (くずはらしんのう) * {{ill|King Takami|ja|高見王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Takami|1=Takami-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672827}} (たかみおう) * {{ill|Taira no Takamochi|en|Taira no Takamochi|qid=Q11483791|lt=King Takamochi|1=Taira no Takamochi}} (たかもちおう) == History == In the 3rd year of {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tenkyo|1=Tengyō}} (940), {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|en|Taira no Yoshifumi|qid=Q11483528|lt=Muraoka Gorō Yoshifumi|1=Taira no Yoshifumi}} enshrined for the purpose of praying for the defeat of {{ill|Taira Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by invoking the enshrinement from the {{ill|Kamigorei Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|lt=Goryō Shrine|lt_ja=御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigorei Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359018}} of Rakuchu ({{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}} {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}} {{ill|Kamigorei-mae Street|ja|上御霊前通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigorei-mae Street|1=Kamigoryōmae Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359017}}). Later, {{ill|Kamakura Kagemasa|ja|鎌倉権五郎景政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉景正|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1154948}}, {{ill|Prince Kuzuhara|ja|葛原親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kuzuhara|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620877}}, {{ill|King Takami|ja|高見王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Takami|1=Takami-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672827}}, and {{ill|King Takamochi|ja|高望王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Takamochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平高望|1=Taira no Takamochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483791}} were added to the enshrined deities. == Subshrines == * {{ill|The Twelve Heavenly Kings|ja|十二天王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Twelve Heavenly Kings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135504457}} * {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|qid=Q7543689|lt=Smallpox Deity|1=Smallpox demon}} * Yatake {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|qid=Q719665|lt=Great Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} – also written as Ori-sasa Yatake Inari or Sasa-ori Yatake Inari. It is said that {{ill|Kamakura Kagemasa|ja|鎌倉権五郎景政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉景正|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1154948}}, during the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|qid=Q1187411|lt=Later Three Years' War|1=Gosannen War}}, had an arrow rebound after one of his eyes was struck, defeating his enemy. When that arrow was driven into the ground, bamboo sprang up, and thus Yatake Inari was enshrined.{{ill|Fujisawa Place Names 3rd Edition|ja|藤沢の地名 第3版|lt=Fujisawa Place Names, 3rd Edition, 1997|lt_ja=『藤沢の地名』1997th year 、第3版。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Place Names 3rd Edition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 99, 100 pages. * {{ill|Seven-Faced Shrine|ja|七面宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven-Faced Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Branch Shrines == Owing to its proximity to the ruins of Muraoka Castle—the former residence of {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|en|Taira no Yoshifumi|qid=Q11483528|lt=Muraoka Gorō Yoshifumi|1=Taira no Yoshifumi}}—located in Muraoka Castle Ruins Park (28-2 Muraoka Higashi 3-chōme, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}) ({{Coord|35|20|40.4|N|139|30|27.8|E|region:JP|name=村岡城址}}), there are 16 shrines in the prefecture said to have branched off from this shrine via {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|qid=Q195793|lt=Branching of Spirit|1=Bunrei}}. These include, for example, the shrine in Kamakura City – {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Kamakura)|ja|御霊神社 (鎌倉市)|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt_ja=御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Kamakura)|1=Goryō Shrine (Kamakura City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489364}}, the one in Hayama Town – {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Hayama)|ja|御霊神社 (葉山町)|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt_ja=御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Hayama)|1=Goryō Shrine (Hayama Town)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489362}}, and Kumano Shrine in Ōoba, {{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}}, also identified with the former {{ill|Ooba Shrine|ja|大庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooba Shrine|1=Ooba Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11435394}}, as well as other shrines of the same lineage such as {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|大庭神社#熊野神社（藤沢市大庭）|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|lt_ja=熊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}. File:Historic site of Muraoka Castle, Fujisawa, Kanagawa.jpg|Muraoka Castle Ruins File:Goryou jinnja 002.jpg|Goryō Shrine (Kamakura City) File:OOBA-Jinjya-kuma03.JPG|Kumano Shrine (Ōoba, Fujisawa City) == Festivals == * Annual Festival: Second Sunday of September * Yuhana Kagura == Location Information == ; Location : {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|en|Fujisawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q386661|lt=Fujisawa City|1=Fujisawa, Kanagawa}} Miyamae 560 ; Transportation :* Bus :** Muraoka Miyamae ({{ill|Enoden Bus|ja|江ノ電バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoden Bus|1=Enoden Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11550595}}) == Other == * Hatadateyama – The hill extending east of Goryō Shrine, also known as Heitan, is so named because {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qid=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} raised a white flag here to rally his troops.{{ill|Fujisawa Place Names 3rd Edition|ja|藤沢の地名 第3版|lt=Fujisawa Place Names, 3rd Edition, 1997|lt_ja=『藤沢の地名』1997th year 、第3版。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Place Names 3rd Edition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 100 pages. == References == '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Note"}} '''Sources''' {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book |language=Japanese |author= |editor=Japan Place Names Institute |title=Fujisawa Place Names |edition=3rd edition |date=1997-03-31 |publisher=Fujisawa City Department of Civic Activities, Local Autonomy and Cultural Affairs |isbn= |ref=Fujisawa Place Names 3rd Edition}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県の神社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588941}} == External Links == {{Osm box|w|258439722}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=514&cd=1206001&scd=&npg=0 {{ill|Goryo Shrine|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo Shrine|qq=}}] - Kanagawa Prefecture Shinto Shrine Office {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御霊神社 (藤沢市宮前)|version=101568321|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御霊神社 (藤沢市宮前)]] [[ja:御霊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q20044399}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fujisawa]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
